---
title: Upstash Adapter
description: Serverless-compatible cache backed by Upstash Redis (HTTP).
icon: Cloud
---

## When to use

Use the Upstash adapter for serverless or edge deployments where you cannot maintain a persistent TCP connection. Upstash Redis communicates over HTTP, making it compatible with Cloudflare Workers, Vercel Edge, and other edge runtimes.

## Installation

```bash
bun add @justwant/cache @upstash/redis
```

## Setup

```ts title="lib/cache.ts"
import { createCache } from "@justwant/cache";
import { createUpstashAdapter } from "@justwant/cache/adapters/upstash";
import { Redis } from "@upstash/redis";

const redis = new Redis({
  url: process.env.UPSTASH_REDIS_REST_URL!,
  token: process.env.UPSTASH_REDIS_REST_TOKEN!,
});

export const cache = createCache({
  adapter: createUpstashAdapter({ client: redis }),
});
```

## Options

```ts
createUpstashAdapter({
  client: Redis;          // @upstash/redis client (required)
  prefix?: string;        // Key prefix applied to all entries
})
```

## Environment variables

```bash
# .env
UPSTASH_REDIS_REST_URL=https://...upstash.io
UPSTASH_REDIS_REST_TOKEN=AX...
```

Get these from the [Upstash Console](https://console.upstash.com).
